reasoning: Paul Biedermann set the 200m long course freestyle record of 1:42.00 in 2009 during the supersuit era. This record has proven highly resilient over the past 16 years. David Popovici, the closest active competitor, has a personal best of 1:42.97 (from 2022) and recently swam a 1:43.53 to win the 2025 World Championships in Singapore. There is a significant performance gap of nearly 1 full second still remaining to break Biedermann's record. Furthermore, the forecast horizon (November 2025 to January 2026) overlaps closely with the typical short-course (25m) swimming season, making it highly unlikely that we will see peak long-course (50m) events where such a historic record could be broken. Therefore, the chance of the record falling during this brief window is extraordinarily low, leaving only minuscule risks such as retroactive administrative actions (which are incredibly unlikely after 16 years). Probabilities start at the allowed maximum of 0.95 and decrease slightly as the time horizon extends.
